dto.creator: achriston

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
• Set up the Standard Neonatal Environment as shown in Chapter 2.1
  • Ensure, all devices are running and connected to the Network
  • Ensure, that the TAI-Server application is running on the notebook connected to the ASL-5000
  • Start the device
  • If not already done run all calibrations and tests in the “Preop check”-tab with the correct humidification type
  • Set the leakage valve to 20% (±5%) by using Vti and Vte of the IMT Flow Analyser (Vte = 0.8 * Vti). (You can run “Vent Mode: Set Leakage for Neonatal” on https://medical-portal.hamilton.ch/apps/ait-mgmt/testCases/66/revisions/0 to set the leakage valve)
  • Run the last released version of the test case “[STBR] PPL.0002 Ventilation Mode (PCV+) neonatal automatic FULL” with the ID 101 on Medical Portal: AIT Management.
  • After the test is executed, export the test results and copy them into the Tables of the test-case.
testSectionDTOList.title: Test Preparation

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the applied PEEP and Pcontrol are tested.

The sequence for this test is:

(ASL) -> (Setup) -> (Exec) -> (Evaluation) -> (CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation
  • Waits for 20 seconds

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Asserts PEEP measured by the ventilator
  • Asserts peak pressure measured by the ventilator
  • Asserts PEEP measured by the flow analyser
  • Asserts peak pressure measured by the flow analyser

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation
testSectionDTOList.title: PEEP and Pcontrol

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the applied rate and inspiration time with TI-philosophy are tested.

The sequence for each partial tests is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for 20 seconds.
  • Stores the inspiration time measured by the flow-analyser in [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER]

(Scripting)-Block: Multiplies the value in [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER] by 1000 and stores it in [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_FACTORED]

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Evaluates the inspiration time measured by the ventilator
  • Evaluates the inspiration time in [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_FACTORED]
  • Evaluates the applied rate measured by the ventilator
  • Evaluates the applied rate measured by the flow-analyser

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: Rate and Inspiration Time with TI-Philosophy

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: Factor Inspiration time by a factor of 1000

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the applied rate and I:E-ratio is tested. For each partial test, the sequence is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for 20 seconds.
  • Stores two measurements of inspiration time and expiration time by the ventilator in the variables [TI_VENTILATOR_1], [TE_VENTILATOR_1], [TI_VENTILATOR_2], [TE_VENTILATOR_2]
  • Stores one measurement of inspiration time and expiration time by the flow-analyser in the variables [TI_FLOWANALYSER], [TE_FLOWANALYSER]
    Note: The block takes two measurements from the ventilator, in case the first one is taken while the controller is auto-zeroing, resulting in an invalid measurement.

(Scripting)-Block:

  • Checks the ventilator measurements from the (Exec)-block for invalid values and substitutes invalid values by a spare measurement, if necessary.
  • Calculates the applied I:E-ratio measured by the ventilator, multiplies it by 100 and stores it in [I_E_VENTILATOR_FACTORED]
  • Calculates the applied I:E-ratio measured by the flow-analyser, multiplies it by 100 and stores it in [I_E_FLOWANALYSER_FACTORED]
    Note: The calculated I:E-ratios are multiplied due to insufficient capabilities of AIT to evaluate floating-point numbers.

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Evaluates the applied I:E-ratio in [I_E_VENTILATOR_FACTORED]
  • Evaluates the applied I:E-ratio in [I_E_FLOWANALYSER_FACTORED]
  • Evaluates the applied Rate measured by the ventilator
  • Evaluates the applied Rate measured by the flow-analyser

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: Rate and I:E-Ratio with I:E-Philosophy

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the effects of the VT-High setting are tested. The test is passed, if

  • an application of 111% of the set VT-High limit causes a "VT-High" alarm, but the ventilation is continued as if no alarm was present.
  • an application of 200% of the set VT-High limit causes an " VT high: breath terminated" alarm
  • the applied tidal volume on an " VT high: breath terminated " alarm is 1.5 times the set "VT-High" limit and the inspiratory time is shorter than before the alarm. (The applied breath gets aborted)

The test uses three control-variables to evaluate the criterions above:

  • [is_valid]: is initially set to 1. If a script finds a value outside of the tolerances, the variable is set to -1
  • [error_code]: is initially set to 200. If [is_valid] = -1, then the value in [error_code] represents the script that reported a failed value
  • [successful_scripts]: counts the number of executed scripts.

Note: The test is executed with R = 20mbar/L/s and C = 5ml/mbar to avoid “VT high: breath terminated” alarm in (Exec |2/3)-Block, which should only appear in (Exec |3/3)-Block.

The sequence for this test is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec |1/3)->(Exec |2/3)->(Exec |3/3)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec |1/3)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for 20 seconds.
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow-analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_START], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_START], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_START], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_START].

(Exec |2/3)-Block:

  • Sets the VT-High alarm to 63ml (approximately 90% of the currently applied expiration volume of 70ml)
  • Waits for the "VT-High" alarm. Note: the internal code for "VT-High" alarm is 141002
  • Waits for 5 seconds
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow-analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE].

(Exec |3/3)-Block:

  • Sets the VT-High alarm to 35ml (approximately 50% of the applied expiration volume of 70ml at the start of the test)
  • Waits for the " VT high: breath terminated " alarm. Note: the internal code for " VT high: breath terminated " alarm is 133002
  • Waits for 10 seconds
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow-analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_END], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_END], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_END], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_END].

(Scripting)-Block:

  • Sets control variables [is_valid] = 1, [error_code] = 200 and [successful_scripts] = 0
  • Evaluates, if the stored varaibles in (Exec |1/3) are the same as in (Exec |2/3)
  • Evaluates, if the stored inspiration time in (Exec |3/3) is smaller than in (Exec |1/3)

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Evaluates, if all scripts in (Scripting) were executed
  • Evaluates, that [is_valid] is still 1
  • Evaluates, that [error_code] is 200
  • Evaluates the applied inspiration volume stored in [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_END]

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.

Check, that Inspiration volume of the first two captures is the same

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the minimal applied expiration time is tested. For each partial test, the sequence is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for 20 seconds.
  • Stores one measurement of the expiration/inspiration time measured by the Flow-Analyser.

(Scripting)-Block: Converts recorded measurement from the Flow-Analyser from “s” to “ms”. Note: The value is multiplied due to insufficient capabilities of AIT to evaluate floating-point numbers.

(Evaluation)-Block: Compares the measurement to the expected value

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: TexpMin

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman: This test shows that the expiration phase of a mandatory machine-triggered breath can be terminated after 0.2 s.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........TexpMin without patient effort (C6-Rate = 80b/min, Tinsp = 0.55s) => Texp = 0.20s

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: Factor Expiration time by a factor of 1000

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
All patient breaths are detected. This shows that the expiration phase of a mandatory patient-triggered breath can be terminated after 0.2 s. 

Here, the defined TI ventilator setting leads to the applied inspiration phase time.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........TexpMin with patient effort (ASL-Rate = 60b/min, Tinsp = 0.80s) => Texp = 0.20s

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: If first measurement is taken, while trigger was not detected, take the second measurement
Factor Expiration time by a factor of 1000

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
Only each 2nd patient effort is detected. This shows that patient efforts inside the TexpMin-window are ineffective. The expected applied expiration phase time is 1.05 s.

Here, the defined TI ventilator setting leads to the applied inspiration phase time.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........TexpMin with ineffective patient effort (ASL-Rate = 60b/min, Tinsp = 0.95s) => Texp = 1.05s
